response:
The fare structure relies on a base charge plus extra costs for distance beyond four kilometers.
Spring brings many travelers to the airport for their journeys to the hotel.
The total cost equals ten yuan plus two yuan for each kilometer over the initial limit.
Any fraction of a kilometer gets rounded up to the next whole number for billing.
This rule ensures that short delays or extra distance increase the final price paid.

The passenger paid thirty-eight yuan which implies the calculated distance was nineteen kilometers.
Spring traffic often causes stops that add to the effective distance traveled by the taxi.
The actual road distance was fifteen kilometers so the extra four kilometers came from waiting time.
Since five minutes of waiting equals one kilometer the driver stopped for twenty minutes total.
This maximum stop time explains the difference between the road distance and the billed distance.
